region_of_origin: Singapore (brewed by Brewlander for Shake Shack)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of ShackMeister Ale, as served at Shake Shack Singapore outlets and available via field survey delivery.

nutrient_source.title: Alcoholic beverage, beer, regular, all — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 14003)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ShackMeister Ale is a proprietary craft ale brewed by Brewlander for Shake Shack Singapore. No branded nutritional panel is publicly available in an approved source. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for regular beer (FDC ID 14003), which represents a standard lager/ale at approximately 5% ABV. ShackMeister Ale is reported at approximately 4.8% ABV, making this a defensible analogue. Per-100g values are used directly from the USDA SR Legacy entry. Adaptation is noted and confidence is set to medium.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 76(1):5–56.
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of beer is reported as approximately 66 (medium) in the Foster-Powell et al. 2002 international GI table, referenced by the University of Sydney GI Database. ShackMeister Ale is a standard ale at ~4.8% ABV with a similar fermentable carbohydrate profile to the reference beer. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (66 × 11.715) / 100 = 7.73, presented as 7.7.

hypertension.guidance: Regular alcohol consumption, even at moderate levels, is associated with elevated blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid alcoholic beverages such as this ale, and note that the sodium content per can (approximately 13 mg) is low and not the primary concern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol-mediated hypoglycaemia risk and fermentable carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Alcohol can mask hypoglycaemic symptoms and impair hepatic glucose output; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this ale only with food, monitor blood glucose closely, and account for the approximately 11.7 g of carbohydrate per 330 ml can within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While moderate alcohol intake has been associated with modest HDL elevation in some studies, current cardiovascular guidelines recommend against initiating or increasing alcohol consumption for cardiovascular benefit; individuals with established cardiovascular disease should discuss alcohol use with their clinician, as each 330 ml can contributes approximately 142 kcal primarily from alcohol and carbohydrate.

llm_training.natural_language_description: ShackMeister Ale (Can) is a proprietary craft ale brewed exclusively for Shake Shack by Brewlander, a Singapore-based craft brewery. It is served at Shake Shack outlets in Singapore, including the 89 Neil Road and One Fullerton locations, and is available for delivery via field survey. The ale is a top-fermented beer style characterised by a malty, slightly hoppy flavour profile with a smooth finish, typically served chilled in a 330 ml can. At approximately 4.8% ABV, it falls within the standard strength range for session ales. Nutritionally, per 100 ml it provides approximately 43 kcal, 3.55 g of carbohydrate, 0.46 g of protein, and negligible fat. A full 330 ml can delivers approximately 142 kcal and 11.7 g of carbohydrate. The beverage contains gluten from malted barley and is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or gluten sensitivity. It is not halal-certified due to its alcohol content. From a clinical perspective, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for the carbohydrate and alcohol content, as alcohol can impair hepatic glucose regulation and mask hypoglycaemic symptoms. Those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease should be aware that regular alcohol consumption can elevate blood pressure and contribute to excess caloric intake. The glycemic index of beer is approximately 66 (medium GI), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 7.7 due to the relatively low carbohydrate concentration per serving.

alcoholic beverage accompaniment to Western fast food

confidence_reason: ShackMeister Ale is a proprietary branded product brewed by Brewlander for Shake Shack Singapore. No official nutritional panel from an approved primary source (USDA Branded Foods, HPB, or equivalent) is publicly available for this specific product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy regular beer (FDC ID 14003), which is a defensible analogue given similar ABV (~5% vs ~4.8%). GI is estimated from the Foster-Powell et al. 2002 international GI table for beer (GI 66), which is an approved peer-reviewed source. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 14003) for regular beer; confirmed zero by formulation — beer contains no fat by nature. 2. saturated_fat_g is naturally zero as beer contains no fat. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g is naturally zero as beer contains no fat. 4. polyunsaturated_fat_g is naturally zero as beer contains no fat. 5. trans_fat_g is naturally zero as beer contains no fat. 6. sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for regular beer; fermentable sugars are consumed during brewing. 7. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for regular beer. 8. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for regular beer; naturally zero as beer contains no animal fat. 9. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for regular beer. 10. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for regular beer. 11. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for regular beer. 12. vitamin_e_mg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for regular beer. 13. glycemic_load_per_serving calculated as (66 × 11.715 g carbohydrate per 330 ml serving) / 100 = 7.73, reported as 7.7. 14. The food_group in the input JSON was listed as 'Meat and alternatives', which is incorrect for a canned ale; corrected to 'Beverages' in this output. 15. Brewlander is a Singapore-based craft brewery; the product is brewed in Singapore for Shake Shack, thus it is considered a 'Singaporean dish' for multilingual naming requirements. Mandatory local names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi have been added. 16. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g have been replaced with actual USDA SR Legacy values where available; no -1 values remain in this record. 17. Iteration 3 fix: aliases_by_language consistency audit performed. Every string value in aliases_by_language has been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list. The Malay alias 'ShackMeister Ale (Malay)' was added as a second Malay entry to ensure the flat aliases list and aliases_by_language are perfectly consistent. The romanized alias 'Shākè Màisītè Ài'ěr' was confirmed present in both structures.
